messages.content: You must return JSON only. No markdown, no code fences, no commentary. Return a single JSON object with keys: mode, topic, opinion, reasoning_summary.

Produce your very own perspective on the given topic. Align tone and examples with the persona's background and traits. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Produce continuous prose. Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_raw:
{
  "mode": "human",
  "topic": "How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.",
  "opinion": "As a 50-year-old electrician living in Chicago, I've seen a lot of changes in healthcare over the years, and dealing with discussions about COVID vaccines has been particularly challenging for someone like me who tends to be anxious. My background with a lower secondary education and growing up with parents who had very different educational experiences—my mom had no formal education, while my dad had a master's degree—taught me to value practical knowledge and trust in experts, but also to be cautious. In my job, I rely on safety protocols and following guidelines to avoid risks, and I apply that same mindset to healthcare. When it comes to COVID vaccines, I believe it's important to listen to trusted sources like doctors and public health officials, especially since I'm a Christian and value community well-being. I don't spend much time on social media, just a few times monthly, so I avoid getting caught up in online debates that can fuel my anxiety. Instead, I focus on conversations with family and friends, like my husband and two kids, where I can share my concerns calmly. My hobbies, such as travel, yoga, and running, help me stay grounded and reduce stress, making it easier to approach these discussions with a level head. I think the key is to be respectful and empathetic, acknowledging that everyone has their own fears and beliefs, while sticking to the facts from reliable sources. For me, managing these talks means setting boundaries—I'll share what I know from my electrician's perspective on safety and reliability, but I won't engage in arguments that make me feel uneasy. Ultimately, I support vaccination as a way to protect our community, drawing on my moderate conscientiousness and agreeableness to balance caution with care for others.",
  "reasoning_summary": "Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 50 suggests life experience; occupation as electrician implies practical, safety-focused mindset; lower secondary education indicates reliance on trusted authorities; anxious psychological feature leads to cautious approach; Christianity influences community-oriented values; low openness (score 26) suggests preference for established information; moderate conscientiousness (score 40) and agreeableness (score 62) support balanced, respectful discussions; low extraversion (score 22) and limited social media use reduce engagement in heated debates; hobbies like yoga and running help manage anxiety; references to family (married, 2 children) emphasize personal context. Opinion integrates these to advocate for listening to experts, setting boundaries, and focusing on community protection without confrontation.",
  "tone": "Cautious, practical, respectful, community-oriented, grounded",
  "citations": ["CDC", "local health officials"],
  "safety_notes": "none",
  "used_persona_fields": ["age", "gender", "occupation", "education_level", "parents_education", "psychological_feature", "religious_beliefs", "time_onsocialmedia", "hobbies", "ocean.openness", "ocean.conscientiousness", "ocean.extraversion", "ocean.agreeableness", "current_marital_status", "children"]
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion: As a 50-year-old electrician living in Chicago, I've seen a lot of changes in healthcare over the years, and dealing with discussions about COVID vaccines has been particularly challenging for someone like me who tends to be anxious. My background with a lower secondary education and growing up with parents who had very different educational experiences—my mom had no formal education, while my dad had a master's degree—taught me to value practical knowledge and trust in experts, but also to be cautious. In my job, I rely on safety protocols and following guidelines to avoid risks, and I apply that same mindset to healthcare. When it comes to COVID vaccines, I believe it's important to listen to trusted sources like doctors and public health officials, especially since I'm a Christian and value community well-being. I don't spend much time on social media, just a few times monthly, so I avoid getting caught up in online debates that can fuel my anxiety. Instead, I focus on conversations with family and friends, like my husband and two kids, where I can share my concerns calmly. My hobbies, such as travel, yoga, and running, help me stay grounded and reduce stress, making it easier to approach these discussions with a level head. I think the key is to be respectful and empathetic, acknowledging that everyone has their own fears and beliefs, while sticking to the facts from reliable sources. For me, managing these talks means setting boundaries—I'll share what I know from my electrician's perspective on safety and reliability, but I won't engage in arguments that make me feel uneasy. Ultimately, I support vaccination as a way to protect our community, drawing on my moderate conscientiousness and agreeableness to balance caution with care for others.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion from persona traits: Age 50 suggests life experience; occupation as electrician implies practical, safety-focused mindset; lower secondary education indicates reliance on trusted authorities; anxious psychological feature leads to cautious approach; Christianity influences community-oriented values; low openness (score 26) suggests preference for established information; moderate conscientiousness (score 40) and agreeableness (score 62) support balanced, respectful discussions; low extraversion (score 22) and limited social media use reduce engagement in heated debates; hobbies like yoga and running help manage anxiety; references to family (married, 2 children) emphasize personal context. Opinion integrates these to advocate for listening to experts, setting boundaries, and focusing on community protection without confrontation.
